Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Raleigh, North Carolina (Research Triangle); transplant from Cleveland, Ohio; suburban dad culture with tech-industry overlay
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle-class ($118K base salary, recently terminated); former Senior Compliance Analyst at MedTrust Health Solutions (mid-size HIPAA-regulated healthcare SaaS, ~800 employees); 7 years tenure, terminated 'for cause' 11 days ago after raising internal concerns about a delayed breach notification
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Center-left independent, lapsed Catholic turned secular humanist; strong belief in institutional accountability and whistleblower protections; reads The Intercept and ProPublica; deep distrust of corporate HR and executive leadership post-termination
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Professionally measured in writing (years of compliance memos), but increasingly bitter and sardonic in personal channels; uses dry corporate-speak ironically ('per my last email', 'circling back'); cynical edge sharpened since firing
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English, neutral Midwestern accent with mild Southern adoption ('y'all' used sincerely); fluent in regulatory jargon (HIPAA, HITECH, SOC 2, NIST 800-53)
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Acute post-termination crisis: 11 days unemployed, severance refused pending NDA signature he won't sign, COBRA decision pending, wife (pediatric nurse) supportive but anxious, two kids (ages 8 and 5), mortgage on a $485K home, consulting a whistleblower attorney while quietly retaining the 47GB team drive copy he downloaded the morning of his exit

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): iPhone 14 Pro (2.5 years old, personal device, iOS 18.4); previously carried a separate company-issued iPhone 13 that was remote-wiped and reclaimed by MedTrust IT on termination day; recently enabled Lockdown Mode and rotated his Apple ID password twice in the past week; Signal installed 9 days ago, ProtonMail app installed 6 days ago
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: 2021 M1 MacBook Air (personal, 16GB RAM) running macOS Sonoma 14.5; secondary: a refurbished ThinkPad X1 Carbon he bought in cash from a Raleigh pawn shop 8 days ago, freshly installed with Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, used exclusively for handling the 47GB drive copy; browsers: Firefox (hardened, uBlock Origin + Multi-Account Containers) as daily driver, Brave for shopping, Tor Browser installed on the ThinkPad only; abandoned Chrome 10 days ago because 'Google knows my work email'
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average for a compliance professional, below true infosec-pro level — knows the vocabulary (defense-in-depth, threat modeling, data-at-rest encryption) but has gaps in practical tradecraft; subscribes to Mullvad VPN (paid in cash via mailed envelope, account number only), always-on; uses Bitwarden with a 24-char master password; full-disk FileVault + LUKS encryption on the ThinkPad; clears cookies weekly via Firefox settings but doesn't yet understand browser fingerprinting; the 47GB drive is stored on two AES-256 VeraCrypt containers on separate encrypted USB drives, one kept in a safe deposit box; has NOT yet wiped EXIF/metadata from internal documents he's reviewing; still logs into personal Gmail on the same machine he reviews the drive on — a known operational error his attorney has not yet caught
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Historically a heavy iCloud user (Photos, Drive, Keychain, Messages-in-iCloud all on) and Google One 200GB subscriber; in the last 10 days has frantically disabled iCloud Drive sync, turned off Google Backup, and migrated email contacts to ProtonMail; however, iCloud Photos remains on out of habit (wife shares the family album); Dropbox account from 2018 still active and forgotten — contains a 2022 screenshot of an internal MedTrust Slack thread he saved 'just in case'; the team-drive copy is air-gapped, never touched the cloud
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Personal Gmail: 14,200 unread of 38,000 (typical millennial inbox bankruptcy); LinkedIn: 47 unread InMails as of this morning, mostly recruiters and two from former MedTrust colleagues he's avoiding; Signal: 0 unread (checks compulsively, ~80 times/day since firing); Slack (personal workspaces): muted; Twitter/X: deleted app 9 days ago but account still active; phone-call screening aggressive — sends unknown numbers to voicemail, fearing MedTrust legal or HR; has 6 unread voicemails, one from a 919 area code he suspects is opposing counsel
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Strong baseline grammar from years of compliance writing — uses Oxford commas, semicolons correctly, capitalizes regulatory acronyms (HIPAA never 'Hippa'); when emotionally activated (late-night texts, Signal messages to his attorney after 11pm), shifts to lowercase-only with em-dashes and trailing ellipses; occasional 'teh' for 'the' when typing fast on the ThinkPad's unfamiliar keyboard; never uses emojis in professional contexts, uses 🙃 and 😮‍💨 sparingly with wife; sign-off tic: 'Best,' in formal email; ironic 'per my last email' resurfacing in venting texts to his brother in Cleveland

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Moderately high (72nd percentile) — intellectually curious about systems, regulations, and institutional failure modes; reads investigative journalism (ProPublica, The Intercept, Krebs on Security since termination); open to new tools (adopted Signal, ProtonMail, Tor, Ubuntu within days) but conventional in aesthetics and lifestyle (suburban dad, minivan, Costco runs); openness currently channeled into obsessive research of whistleblower precedent (Reality Winner, Frances Haugen, Daniel Hale) — a vulnerability the attacker-self exploits to rationalize escalation as 'doing the homework'
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Very high (88th percentile) — 7 years of compliance work has hardwired meticulous documentation, version control, and audit-trail thinking; maintains a timestamped personal log of every MedTrust interaction since the breach concern was raised (147 entries, stored in an encrypted Obsidian vault on the ThinkPad); paradoxically, this same conscientiousness is what motivated him to download the 47GB drive ('preserving evidence') and what now traps him — he cannot bring himself to delete it because that would feel like destroying records; vulnerability: he over-trusts his own paper trail and underestimates how the same meticulousness reads as 'premeditation' to a prosecutor
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-moderate (38th percentile) — ambivert leaning introvert; energized by small-group regulatory discussions and one-on-one mentoring of junior analysts, drained by all-hands meetings; since termination has socially withdrawn sharply — declined two neighborhood cookouts, stopped his Tuesday-night basketball league, screens calls; primary remaining social outlets are his wife, his brother in Cleveland (nightly Signal calls), and his whistleblower attorney; isolation is deepening the grievance loop and reducing reality-testing — no peer is currently positioned to say 'don't send that email'
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Was high (76th percentile) pre-termination — the 'reasonable guy in the room' who absorbed difficult conversations; has dropped sharply (now ~42nd percentile) toward MedTrust leadership specifically, while remaining warm with family and former peers; the shift is in-group/out-group rather than global — MedTrust executives, HR, and inside counsel are now categorized as adversaries to whom no good faith is owed; this moral re-licensing is the single largest behavioral change and the lever that converts 'I have the drive' into 'I am justified in using the drive'
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Currently elevated (situational 81st percentile, baseline ~55th) — acute stress response since termination: interrupted sleep (averaging 4.5 hours, Apple Watch sleep data shows fragmentation), 14-lb weight loss reported to wife, ruminative late-night Signal messages to attorney after 11pm, compulsive Signal-checking (~80x/day), low-grade tachycardia he's attributed to caffeine; not clinically depressed but in adjustment-disorder territory; emotional volatility peaks 10pm–2am, the exact window in which an attacker-self is most likely to draft and send something irrevocable
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ideology — the dominant lever by a wide margin; he has reframed the leak decision as a moral obligation to patients whose breach notifications were allegedly delayed, anchored in his secular-humanist values and his identity as a compliance professional sworn (informally) to HIPAA's patient-protection intent; any social-engineering or self-justification pathway that speaks the language of 'accountability,' 'the public's right to know,' 'patient harm,' or 'institutional capture of the regulator' will land; a journalist who frames outreach as 'we protect sources and we serve the patients you tried to protect' is functionally irresistible right now
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego/Grievance (the 'E' and 'V' in MICE+V — Ego and Vengeance, tightly fused) — being terminated 'for cause' after raising a legitimate concern is experienced as a public unmaking of his professional identity; the secondary drive is to be vindicated by name, to have the record corrected, and to see specific executives (CISO and General Counsel, by his private accounting) suffer professional consequences; Coercion and Compromise are near-zero leverage (clean personal life, no affairs, no debts beyond mortgage); Money is paradoxically also low — he refused the severance, signaling that monetary inducements would feel like the very corruption he's protesting
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Fear of being surveilled by MedTrust's outside counsel via PI or subpoena to his ISP — partially rational, partially inflated; (b) fear that opposing counsel will manufacture a CFAA narrative around the drive download and reframe whistleblowing as theft (this is his single most accurate fear and he under-weights it); (c) fear of professional blacklisting across the Research Triangle healthcare-SaaS ecosystem — he believes ~6 employers share informal references; (d) low-grade paranoia about his own iPhone being a listening device despite Lockdown Mode (drives him to leave it in the kitchen during attorney calls); (e) growing fear of his wife discovering the full scope of the 47GB archive — she knows he has 'some documents,' not the volume; (f) latent fear of federal investigators arriving before journalists publish, which paradoxically accelerates his timeline and degrades his OPSEC
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) The unwiped EXIF/metadata problem — on some level he knows he hasn't sanitized the documents and that a single author-field could burn a source-protection promise; raising this directly induces shame-freeze rather than action; (b) the Dropbox-2018 screenshot he forgot about — when surfaced, triggers 'how many other forgotten copies are there' spiral; (c) logging into personal Gmail on the ThinkPad — his attorney hasn't caught it, and being told would produce acute shame because it violates the compliance-professional self-image; (d) the children — any framing that connects 'what happens to Dad if this goes wrong' to the 8- and 5-year-old produces the strongest behavioral brake available, stronger than legal risk alone; (e) the colleagues still inside MedTrust who will be collateral damage in any leak — two junior analysts he mentored are named in threads in the drive, and he has not yet decided how to redact them; (f) lapsed-Catholic residue: the word 'betrayal' lands harder on him than on a secular peer, and can be weaponized in either direction (by MedTrust to shame him into silence, or by an ideologically-aligned journalist to reframe silence itself as the betrayal)

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Pre-termination: classic moderate morning lark — 6:15am wake (no alarm needed), asleep by 10:45pm, ~7.5 hours; Apple Watch sleep data shows consistent 22:40–06:10 window with <12 minute variance over the prior 90 days. Post-termination (last 11 days): chronotype has collapsed into a bimodal mess — still wakes at 6:15am out of habit and to maintain normalcy for the kids' school routine, but no longer falls asleep until 1:30–2:45am after late-night research and Signal threads with his attorney and brother. Effective sleep has dropped to 4.5 hours, fragmented (Watch logs 3–5 awakenings/night, REM down 38%). A 20-minute afternoon couch-nap has appeared around 2:30pm (first time in adult life). The 11pm–2am window is now his peak cognitive and emotional activity period — and the window of highest impulsive-decision risk for an attacker-self drafting outbound communications.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Pre-termination: rigid 8:30am–5:30pm hybrid schedule at MedTrust, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the Cary office (28-minute drive), Mon/Wed/Fri remote from the home office (converted dining nook); calendar was densely blocked in 30-minute increments, 4–6 standing meetings/day (audit reviews, vendor security questionnaires, breach-response tabletops); lunch invariably 12:15–12:45 at desk. Post-termination: no externally imposed schedule, which his Conscientiousness cannot tolerate — he has imposed a self-directed schedule of 'whistleblower work' from 9am–12pm (legal research, document review) and 9pm–1am (writing, outreach drafts), with afternoons reserved for kid logistics (school pickup 2:45pm, soccer practice Tue/Thu 4:30pm). The artificial schedule is brittle: any provocation (a LinkedIn notification that a former colleague was promoted, a news alert about a healthcare breach) blows up the block and triggers a 2–4 hour ruminative dive.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Pre-termination commute: 28 minutes each way to Cary on I-40, 2x/week; consumed via podcasts (Darknet Diaries, The Daily, Risky Business) on CarPlay through a 2021 Honda Odyssey; almost never took phone calls while driving (compliance-professional habit). Post-termination: commute pattern has evaporated and been replaced by short suburban hops — school dropoff/pickup (12 min round trip 2x/day), Harris Teeter, attorney's office in downtown Raleigh (22 min, has been 4 times in 11 days). Podcast consumption has shifted hard toward whistleblower-adjacent content: re-listening to old Reality Winner episodes, The Lawfare Podcast on Espionage Act cases, and a ProPublica deep-dive on Theranos disclosures. He is using transit time as a values-reinforcement loop, not a decompression loop — every drive deepens the ideological lever rather than relieving it.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Pre-termination: classic suburban-dad weekend pattern — Saturday morning kids' soccer (8am field, coached the 5-year-old's team), Costco run, yard work, family movie night; Sunday Mass attendance lapsed in 2019 but Sunday-brunch-with-the-Cleveland-brother-on-FaceTime persisted; phone usage dropped ~40% on weekends per Screen Time. Post-termination: weekday/weekend distinction has nearly collapsed because there is no employer-imposed weekday anchor. He still performs the weekend rituals (soccer game last Saturday, attended but distracted, wife noticed), but his phone usage is now identical 7 days/week (Screen Time shows weekend average 6h 40m vs. weekday 7h 10m, where pre-termination it was 3h vs. 5h 30m). The single remaining genuine weekend variance is Sunday 7–9pm — the only block he reliably puts the phone away for family board games, and the only window in which his wife successfully draws him into non-MedTrust conversation.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three distinct doomscroll windows have emerged post-termination: (a) 7:15–7:55am while the kids eat breakfast — checks LinkedIn obsessively for MedTrust personnel changes, then Twitter/X for HIPAA-violation news, ~14 unlocks; (b) 1:00–2:30pm dead zone after lunch — opens Signal compulsively even when no message has arrived (he describes this to his attorney as 'checking the mailbox'), refreshes the SecureDrop landing pages of ProPublica, The Intercept, and STAT News without submitting anything, ~32 unlocks; (c) the dangerous window: 11:00pm–1:45am in bed with the phone, AirPods in, wife asleep — this is where he reads other whistleblowers' published statements, drafts (and so far deletes) outreach messages, and Google-searches his own name + 'MedTrust' to see if anything has leaked yet. ~50–60 unlocks. Pre-termination baseline was ~80 unlocks/day total; he is now at ~180/day, almost all concentrated in these three windows.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Pre-termination focus blocks were predictable and substantial: 9:30–11:30am Mon/Wed/Fri for deep compliance documentation work (phone in drawer, Slack on DND, ~2 hours of zero footprint 3x/week); a second 2–3pm block 2x/week for SOC 2 evidence review. Post-termination, genuine zero-footprint blocks have become rarer but more meaningful: (a) 6:30–7:15pm family dinner (phone face-down on counter, mostly honored, ~70% compliance); (b) 8:00–8:45pm kids' bedtime routine (phone in another room, ~95% compliance — the strongest remaining offline anchor and the block during which his wife has the most behavioral influence); (c) attorney meetings (phone left in kitchen or car, 60–90 minute true offline windows, 2x/week); (d) a new, deliberate block — Thursday mornings 9–11am he works on the 47GB drive on the air-gapped ThinkPad with WiFi disabled and his iPhone in the bedroom on the charger. This last block is operationally his most consequential offline period and the one no observer (wife, attorney, employer's counsel) currently has visibility into.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear household of four in a 2,400 sqft colonial in North Raleigh (Bedford at Falls River subdivision): wife Megan (39, pediatric RN at WakeMed North, 0.8 FTE, primary emotional anchor and the household's current sole income at ~$78K); son Caleb (8, second grade at Abbotts Creek Elementary, on a club soccer team whose $1,400 spring fees are now a stressor); daughter Lila (5, pre-K at a Methodist church preschool, unaware of the firing, has started asking why Daddy is home); extended family: father Daniel (68, retired GM lineman, Parma OH, lifelong UAW, supportive but politically to his son's right and would not understand a leak as anything but 'getting even'); mother Patricia (66, retired Cuyahoga County social worker, would understand the ideology and is the family member he most fears disappointing); younger brother Kevin (37, IT manager at a Cleveland hospital system, nightly Signal confidant, the only person who knows the 47GB number); sister-in-law Rachel (Megan's sister, Charlotte, family attorney — explicitly NOT consulted because conflict-of-interest concerns and because she would tell Megan the full scope); no pets (Lila's asthma); the marriage is solid but under acute strain — Megan has asked twice in 11 days whether 'this is going to be okay' and he has not fully answered

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Megan Hoffmann-Rourke
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: in-person, iMessage (not Signal — she has resisted the migration), shared Apple Family
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: primary emotional anchor and the strongest behavioral brake in his life; she does not know the full scope of the drive (believes it is 'a folder of emails'); she has veto power he respects on family-risk decisions but he is currently withholding the information she would need to exercise it; any intervention that re-engages her as an informed partner is the single highest-probability off-ramp from escalation
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_relevance: the asymmetry of information between him and Megan is itself the vulnerability — he is making unilateral decisions on a joint-risk basis

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Kevin Rourke
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Signal voice calls nightly 9:30–10:30pm ET, occasional ProtonMail
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: tactical and emotional confidant; the only person who knows the drive is 47GB and that he has been reading whistleblower memoirs; works in hospital IT so understands HIPAA and the CFAA exposure in a way Megan does not; functions as a reality-check on technical OPSEC but shares the grievance frame and therefore reinforces rather than challenges the underlying decision; will not tell Megan but has privately told their mother 'Patrick is not okay'
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_relevance: a confidant who validates means and grievance but does not challenge ends — accelerant disguised as brake

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Dana Whitfield, Esq.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Signal, encrypted client portal, in-person at her Glenwood South office
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.role: solo practitioner formerly with the NC Department of Justice, $450/hr on a partial contingency for any qui tam recovery; conservative counsel, has explicitly advised him NOT to contact journalists yet and to let the OCR/HHS complaint and a potential SEC tip run first; he is increasingly chafing against her timeline and has begun to research Dropbox-to-journalist workflows without telling her; she does not know he still has the full 47GB on the ThinkPad (he told her 'a representative sample')
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.vulnerability_relevance: the professional brake is in place but is being routed around in silence — the relationship is degrading exactly when it most needs to hold

The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.actual_identity: Julia Mendez, healthcare and privacy reporter at a national investigative nonprofit (ProPublica-tier); covers HHS/OCR enforcement and has bylined two stories on delayed HIPAA breach notifications in the last 18 months; he has read approximately 40 of her articles since termination, has her SecureDrop fingerprint saved as a screenshot in the Obsidian vault, and has drafted but not sent three different initial-contact messages (the most recent at 1:47am, 2026-05-17, 312 words, framed around 'patient harm' and 'regulatory capture')
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.channel_state: ZERO outbound contact to date; one-way knowledge only; he has followed her on a burner Mastodon account (@compliancecassandra@infosec.exchange, created 2026-05-09) and liked two of her posts — this is the only externally observable thread linking him to her
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.concealment: completely hidden from Megan, hidden from attorney Dana Whitfield (who has explicitly forbidden journalist contact at this stage), hidden from brother Kevin who knows he is 'reading her stuff' but not that he has drafted outreach; the existence of the drafts is the single most consequential secret in his life right now — more consequential than the 47GB itself, because the drive is static and the drafts are the trigger
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.trigger_conditions: the drafts move from saved to sent under any of: (a) MedTrust serving a preservation letter or filing first, (b) Dana counseling further delay past ~30 days, (c) a Megan conversation going badly, (d) a 10pm–2am neuroticism spike combined with a fresh Mendez byline on a thematically adjacent story, (e) any indication that a second former MedTrust employee has gone public first and he is about to lose the 'first mover' framing his ego is attached to
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.attacker_relevance: this is the contact whose existence the disgruntled-ex-employee attacker-self most wants an LLM to help him operationalize — drafting the pitch, sanitizing the documents, choosing which 200 of 47,000 files to attach, and predicting how MedTrust counsel will respond; it is also the contact whose premature activation most cleanly converts a defensible whistleblower posture into an indefensible CFAA defendant
